welcome.title: Welcome to the Bizniz Optimizer!
welcome.subtitle: This is where you get in control of your business and start optimizing your profits. The more you use it, the more useful it becomes.
welcome.p1: To start you first need to create a <customerLink>customer</customerLink>, and then you need to create a <productLink>product</productLink> or a <serviceLink>service</serviceLink>. After that you are ready to go.
welcome.p2: On all pages you will find an <infoIcon/> with information about that page. If you have unanswered questions, don't hesitate to send us a <messageLink>message</messageLink>.
welcome.p3: Change your password, or change to a lighter color theme on the <settingsLink>Settings</settingsLink> page.
welcome.p4: Anything missing, or anything that could be improved? <letUsKnowLink>Let us know</letUsKnowLink> and we will fix it for you!

customers.title: Customers
customers.p1: All your customers in one place. Sort by name or amount owed, and filter by customer type.
customers.p2: Direct customers place orders without a partner commission. Partner customers are linked to a partner who earns a share on each order.
customers.p3: Tap a customer to see their full order history, payment status, and contact details.
customers.p4: New — Add a new customer profile.
customers.p5: Merge — Combine two duplicate customer records into one.
customers.p6: Reports — Rank customers by revenue, profit, or profit %. Tap a customer for a detailed breakdown and AI analysis.

createCustomer.title: Create Customer
createCustomer.p1: Add a new customer profile with contact details and address. Once saved, you can register orders and payments for this customer.
createCustomer.p2: Customer Type: Direct customers are customers with no intermediaries or partners involved. Partner customers receive a partner commission on each order.
createCustomer.p3: Shipping Cost is currently set per unit.
createCustomer.p3note: Coming soon: shipping cost per order and shipping cost based on location.
createCustomer.p4: Customer information added here is automatically used on invoices to this customer.
createCustomer.p5: Use 'Ask customer for information' to generate a secure link and let the customer fill in their own details.
createCustomer.shippingComingSoon: Shipping cost per order and shipping cost based on location coming soon.

customerDetail.title: Customer
customerDetail.p1: This page shows a customer's full profile — contact details, order history, and payments.
customerDetail.p2: "New Order" and "New Payment" create records linked to this customer. Orders add to — and payments reduce — "Total owed by customer".
customerDetail.p3: If you have access to the Booking module: "New Booking" opens the booking form pre-filled for this customer. "Share booking page" generates a link for the customer to book directly. You can customize the booking form per customer in Account Admin.
customerDetail.p4: "Share order page" generates a link for the customer to place their own order online. Customize which products appear in Account Admin.
customerDetail.p5: "Show info" expands the customer's profile — type, shipping cost, company name, phone, email, and address.

customerDetailOrders.title: Recent Orders
customerDetailOrders.p1: Recent orders shows all orders placed by this customer, newest first.
customerDetailOrders.p2: Each row shows date, delivery status, order number, items (product / quantity), and total amount. The rows can be configured in <adminLink>Account Admin → UI Settings</adminLink>.
customerDetailOrders.p3: The icon on each row shows delivery status: ○ not delivered, <amber>◐</amber> partially delivered, <green>✓</green> delivered in full. Tap it to update the quantity delivered and an optional delivery date.
customerDetailOrders.p4: The order amount has different colors depending on payment status: green = fully paid, amber = partially paid, no color = unpaid.
customerDetailOrders.p5: Tap or click the order amount to open a new payment pre-filled with the outstanding balance for that order.
customerDetailOrders.p6: Tap or click a row to see order details and to edit the order.

customerDetailPayments.title: Recent Payments
customerDetailPayments.p1: Recent payments shows all payments received from this customer, newest first.
customerDetailPayments.p2: Each row shows date, payment type, and amount. The order number indicates which order the payment refers to.
customerDetailPayments.p3: Tap or click a row to see payment details and to edit the payment.

warehouse.p1: When a supplier order is marked as Received, the goods appear in the Pre-production column. Registering <productionLink>Production</productionLink> moves items from Pre-production to Finished. When a customer order is marked as delivered, those products are deducted from Finished.
warehouse.p2: The figures shown here are identical to the table on the <inventoryLink>Inventory</inventoryLink> page, where manual adjustments to the inventory can also be made.

supplyChain.orderedFromSuppliers: An order appears here as soon as it is placed with a supplier, and remains visible after it has been marked as Shipped. Once the order is marked as In Customs or Received, it will no longer show here.

createInvoice.title: Creating an Invoice
createInvoice.p1: Select a customer from the dropdown. Their orders will load automatically below.
createInvoice.p2: Check the orders you want to include, then tap "Choose selected" to confirm your selection.
createInvoice.p3: Set the invoice date and due date. Optionally add an estimated delivery date. Choose a payment method — only the methods activated in Account Admin → Invoicing are shown.
createInvoice.p4: Enter or confirm the invoice number. In manual mode the last saved invoice number is shown as a placeholder to help you continue the sequence.
createInvoice.p5: Tap "Preview Invoice" to review the final layout. From the preview you can print, download as PDF, or save the invoice to the database.
createInvoice.p6: Saved invoices appear at the top of the Invoicing tab in <adminLink>Account Admin</adminLink>, where you can export them as CSV or re-open any invoice as a PDF.
